data.title: Bitcoin did not experience a six-month consecutive decline, with a return of 1.62% in March.
data.description: Coinglass data shows that Bitcoin's March 2026 return reached 1.62%, avoiding a six-month consecutive decline. In Bitcoin's history, since 2013, there have been 14 instances of a "March rally," with 7 showing gains and 7 showing losses. The largest gain occurred in March 2013, with a monthly increase of 172.76%; the largest loss occurred in March 2018, with a monthly decrease of 32.85%. Furthermore, Bitcoin's first-quarter investment return was -22.34%, marking the third-largest quarterly decline since 2013. Regarding Ethereum, ETH's March return reached 6.97%, avoiding a seven-month consecutive decline. In Ethereum's history, since 2016, there have been 11 instances of a "March rally," with 8 showing gains and 3 showing losses. Furthermore, Ethereum's first-quarter investment return was -29.26%, marking the third-largest quarterly decline since 2017.

data.title: Crypto stocks closed higher across the board in the US market, with ABTC rising over 17.01%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 2.49%, the S&P 500 rose 2.91%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 3.83%. The crypto sector saw widespread gains, with ABTC rising over 17.01%, COIN rising over 8.6%, BMNR rising over 8.09%, SBET rising over 7.32%, and CRCL rising over 6.12%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Gold rose to $4,706, European stock indices generally rose, and volatility indices fell.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices rose to $4,706.39 per ounce, a daily increase of 0.83%. Silver prices rose to $74.808 per ounce, a daily decrease of 0.35%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 53.64, a daily decrease of 2.05%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 72.80, a daily decrease of 3.19%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate fell 0.05% to 6.88299. The USD/JPY exchange rate remained unchanged at 158.708. In global stock indices, the Euro Stoxx 50 rose 2.61% to 5,652.07 points; the UK FTSE 100 rose 0.04% to 10,284.62 points; and the German DAX 40 rose 0.22% to 23,074.62 points. In commodities, WTI crude oil rose 0.68% to $102.65 per barrel, while Brent crude oil rose 1.07% to $108.31 per barrel. Gate allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products on its platform, providing a one-stop solution for precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: The issuance of Hong Kong's first batch of compliant stablecoin licenses has been delayed; the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) responded that it is making every effort to expedite the process.
data.description: The first batch of stablecoin issuer licenses in Hong Kong, originally scheduled for March 2026, has not yet been issued as planned. A spokesperson for the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) responded that the HKMA is fully committed to the licensing process and will announce details in due course. Regarding who will receive the first batch of stablecoin licenses in Hong Kong, the market previously focused on the two major Hong Kong dollar note-issuing banks, HSBC and Standard Chartered Bank. HSBC has not publicly disclosed whether it has submitted an application for a stablecoin license. However, as early as mid-January this year, rumors circulated that HSBC had a significant chance of obtaining one of the first licenses. Currently, there is no official disclosure as to why the stablecoin license issuance has been delayed. Sources close to the stablecoin license application process revealed that the HKMA has recently been in close contact with potential compliant issuers, and suggestions for modifications regarding issuance are still being made. In addition, a second batch of compliant stablecoin licenses in Hong Kong is also under application. Reliable sources revealed that Futu Securities and OSL Group are strong contenders for the second batch of licenses. (Caixin)

data.title: New Hampshire plans to issue $100 million in Bitcoin-backed bonds with a Ba2 rating.
data.description: The New Hampshire Department of Business Finance plans to issue approximately $100 million in Bitcoin-backed municipal bonds, which have been rated Ba2 by Moody's. Repayment of the bonds will rely on funds generated from the Bitcoin collateral, and will not be backed by state credit or tax revenue. The report states that the product may offer additional returns if the price of Bitcoin rises, and includes a price-triggered liquidation mechanism to guarantee principal and interest payments. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Sources say SpaceX is partnering with 21 banks to prepare for its IPO.
data.description: SpaceX is partnering with at least 21 banks to prepare for its blockbuster IPO, one of the largest underwriting syndicates in recent years, sources familiar with the matter revealed on Tuesday. Internally codenamed "Apex," the IPO, expected in June, is projected to value the company at $1.75 trillion and is poised to become one of Wall Street's most anticipated stock market debuts. Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, and Citigroup are acting as active bookrunners, the banks leading the deal, the sources said. They added that another 16 banks have signed on for smaller roles. About half of these banks have not been previously reported on. The size of the underwriting syndicate underscores the scale and complexity of the proposed offering. (Jinshi)

data.title: Cardano's founder criticized the CLARITY bill for its design flaws, saying it could be politicized.
data.description: Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson criticized the U.S. Digital Asset Markets Clarity Act, stating that it has design flaws, could take up to 15 years to implement, and may be "tooled" due to changes in political parties. He pointed out that the FTX incident exacerbated the partisan divide in the U.S. regarding crypto regulation, hindering legislative progress. Hoskinson also stated that the act might default to classifying new projects as securities, thus creating a relative advantage for existing projects, while ignoring regulatory frameworks in Europe (MiCA) and regions like Singapore and Japan, potentially leading to incompatibility with international standards. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Japan's Manufacturing Confidence Rises Amid Middle East Crisis
data.description: Despite the ongoing Middle East crisis, confidence among Japan's large manufacturing enterprises has improved for the fourth consecutive quarter, supporting the Bank of Japan's stance on potential interest rate hikes. According to Jin10, the Bank of Japan's Tankan survey released on Wednesday showed that the business sentiment index for March increased to 17 from a revised 16 in the previous quarter. The index for large non-manufacturing businesses remained steady at 36, near its strongest level since 1991. However, the outlook for large enterprises has weakened, with the manufacturing sentiment index slightly declining to 14 and non-manufacturing dropping from a revised 31 to 29. The Tankan survey is one of the most closely watched indicators by the Bank of Japan, indicating that Japanese business optimism remains stable for now. If the Bank of Japan decides to raise interest rates after the impact of the Iran conflict becomes clearer, this could be one of the factors cited. Traders currently estimate a 69% probability of a rate hike later this month.

data.title: JGBs Increase Amid Optimism for Swift Resolution in Middle East Conflict
data.description: Japanese Government Bonds (JGBs) experienced an uptick as investors expressed optimism for a rapid conclusion to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the market's positive response to potential diplomatic efforts aimed at resolving tensions in the region. Analysts suggest that the anticipation of a peaceful resolution has bolstered investor confidence, leading to increased demand for JGBs. This development comes as geopolitical uncertainties continue to influence global financial markets, with investors closely monitoring the situation for further updates.

data.title: KOSPI Rises 4.52%, Triggers Buy-Side Sidecar
data.description: The KOSPI index experienced a significant increase, rising by 4.52% to reach 5,281.04. According to NS3.AI, this surge led to the activation of a buy-side sidecar. Yonhap reported that this was the first activation of its kind in approximately 20 days, with the last occurrence on March 10.

data.title: Trump to Address Nation on Iran Situation
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is scheduled to deliver a national address concerning the situation with Iran. According to NS3.AI, White House spokesperson Leavitt announced that the address will take place at 1:00 a.m. UTC on April 2. Leavitt emphasized the significance of the update, describing it as an important communication from the President.

data.title: Cathie Wood Adds OpenAI to ARK Investment Funds
data.description:
Cathie Wood has incorporated OpenAI into the ARKK, ARKF, and ARKW funds, according to ChainCatcher. Each fund will hold approximately a 3% stake, allowing retail investors to directly invest in one of the world's most sought-after private companies.

Ark Invest had previously announced its holding in OpenAI through its ARK Venture Fund, a private VC fund, in 2024 and has increased its stake multiple times, including a $250 million investment in October 2024.

data.title: OpenClaw Releases New Version with Enhanced Features
data.description:
OpenClaw has launched its 2026.3.31 version, introducing several new features. According to ChainCatcher, the update includes QQ bot integration for private, group, and guild chats, as well as media sharing on LINE. It also offers real-time task flow management, allowing users to list, view, and cancel tasks, thereby improving automation reliability.

The update focuses on expanding into the Asian market, enhancing support for Chinese, Japanese, and Korean languages, memory, and text-to-speech capabilities. Additionally, it includes WhatsApp emoji reactions and Matrix room history streaming, aiming to broaden communication channels beyond Slack and Discord.

The community has largely praised the update, though some users reported excessive Exec approval prompts and occasional crashes or instability following the update.

data.title: South Korea's Export Growth Surges Amid Semiconductor Demand and Geopolitical Tensions
data.description: South Korea's exports continued to rise significantly in March, driven by strong semiconductor demand, providing a buffer for the economy despite increased external risks from the conflict in Iran. According to Jin10, data released by South Korean customs showed that exports, adjusted for working day differences, soared by 41.9% year-on-year. Unadjusted exports grew by 48.3%, surpassing February's revised monthly increase of 28.7%. Imports rose by 13.2%, resulting in a trade surplus of $25.74 billion. The data indicates that South Korea's export engine remains robust despite mounting challenges from rising energy prices and geopolitical uncertainties. The conflict in Iran has pushed up crude oil prices, increasing import costs and inflation risks for the economy, which heavily relies on overseas energy supplies.

data.title: Ripple Unlocks 500 Million XRP from Escrow
data.description: Ripple has unlocked 500 million XRP from escrow, according to NS3.AI. This move is part of Ripple's regular release schedule, which aims to provide liquidity and support the XRP ecosystem. The unlocking of XRP from escrow is a significant event for the cryptocurrency market, as it can influence the supply and demand dynamics of XRP. Ripple's escrow system is designed to ensure a controlled release of XRP, helping to stabilize its price and prevent market manipulation. The release of XRP from escrow is closely monitored by investors and analysts, as it can impact market sentiment and trading strategies.

data.title: Bitcoin Spot CVD Chart Analysis: Key Support and Resistance Levels
data.description: The BTC spot CVD chart employs a volume heatmap and a CVD indicator to monitor BTC/USDT spot trading activities. According to NS3.AI, this includes transactions ranging from $100 to $1,000 and from $1 million to $10 million. The brighter zones on the heatmap can serve as potential support or resistance levels, especially when the price remains within a range for an extended period or experiences significant movement.

data.title: SpaceX Forms Banking Syndicate for IPO Management
data.description: SpaceX has reportedly established a syndicate of 21 banks to oversee its initial public offering (IPO), according to Jin10. This move marks a significant step for the aerospace company as it prepares to enter the public market. The formation of the banking syndicate is expected to facilitate the IPO process, ensuring efficient management and execution. SpaceX's decision to collaborate with multiple banks highlights its strategic approach to navigating the complexities of going public. Further details about the IPO timeline and specifics have yet to be disclosed.

data.title: Asian Markets Surge Following U.S. Stock Rally
data.description:
Asian stock markets experienced significant gains on April 1, following a strong performance in U.S. markets overnight. According to BlockBeats, South Korea's KOSPI index opened with an increase of 267.92 points, marking a 5.3% rise to 5320.38 points. Similarly, Japan's Nikkei 225 index saw an opening surge of 883.17 points, a 1.73% increase, reaching 51946.89 points.

In the commodities market, spot gold prices rose to $4700 per ounce early on Wednesday, marking a 0.7% increase. This is the first time in nearly two weeks that gold has reached this price level.

data.title: NBA Regular Season: Pistons vs. Raptors Prediction Market Activity
data.description:
A significant transaction has occurred in the Polymarket prediction event for the NBA regular season game between the Detroit Pistons and the Toronto Raptors. According to Odaily, an account with profits exceeding $1.6 million purchased $110,000 worth of shares predicting the Pistons would win with a 3.5-point spread, at an opening price of 48 cents.

The game between the Pistons and Raptors is scheduled to start at 8 a.m. UTC+8. As the NBA regular season nears its conclusion, the Pistons hold a record of 54 wins and 21 losses, placing them first in the Eastern Conference. Meanwhile, the Raptors have a record of 42 wins and 32 losses, ranking fifth in the same conference.

data.title: South Korea's March Trade Surplus Exceeds Expectations
data.description: South Korea's trade surplus for March reached $25.74 billion, surpassing the anticipated $21.2 billion, according to Jin10. The previous figure was revised from $15.51 billion to $15.376 billion. This unexpected increase in the trade surplus highlights the country's strong export performance during the month.
